clauses.body: {{scopeText}}

Work shall conform to the codes and standards governing this project: {{code.fbc}}; {{code.fmc}}; {{code.fecc}}; {{code.nec}}; NFPA 90A; and SMACNA duct construction standards. All equipment UL-listed.
clauses.note: All governing codes enumerated, from STATE_TABLE + standards.

clauses.body: The General Conditions of the Contract govern coordination, bidding, site examination, and construction-phase administration. The Engineer's scope is design and permit; construction-phase services are not included. Drawings are schematic; provide all labor and materials to complete the work shown.
clauses.note: Single paragraph replacing ~8 old contract-admin clauses.

clauses.body: Submit product data, shop drawings, and O&M data. Named products establish a standard of quality; substitutions are permitted if the Contractor demonstrates equal or better performance against every stated requirement.

Systems are sized to: outdoor {{loadcalc.outdoorDB}} °F DB / {{loadcalc.outdoorWB}} °F WB; indoor {{loadcalc.indoorDB}} °F. Equipment shall meet the design loads established by the Engineer's calculations.

Maintain as-built records of concealed work, including buried utility dimensions from building walls; deliver at closeout. Warrant work free of defects for one year from final acceptance. Provide {{boundCopies}} bound set(s) of warranties, manuals, and parts lists.

Support all equipment, ductwork, and piping from the building structure — not from the ceiling or ceiling suspension system. Provide supports rated for the load, sized and spaced per SMACNA and manufacturer requirements.
Provide permanent metal identification tags with engraved unit/tag numbers for new equipment, piping, and valves. Mark concealed condensate terminations as primary or secondary.
Test all mechanical systems per {{code.fmc}}; obtain inspector approval.
{{tbModeClause}}
clauses.note: tbMode 3-way. REQUIRE → 'An independent AABC/NEBB agency shall balance all HVAC equipment.' RECOMMEND → 'Independent AABC/NEBB balancing is recommended.' OMIT → drops. Default recommend.

clauses.body: Insulate ductwork and condensate piping where required to prevent condensation and meet {{code.fecc}}. Insulation, fittings, and finishes: flame-spread ≤ 25, smoke-developed ≤ 50, per NFPA 90A.
Provide a programmable thermostat for each {{thermostatScope}} air-conditioning system. Mount sensors at 4'-6" AFF unless noted; coordinate locations and finishes.
Interlock exhaust and outdoor-air fans with the air handler serving the space.
Control 120-volt restroom exhaust fans (≤ 800 W) by occupancy sensor with adjustable time delay, initially 20 minutes.
Provide a time clock for outdoor-air fans; coordinate occupancy schedules with the Owner.
Integrate new equipment and controls into the existing control system, including all associated costs; provide a complete control description with schematics.

Air-handling systems exceeding 2000 CFM shall shut down automatically on smoke detection (supply after filters/before branches; return before exhaust/OA connections), with one manual emergency shutoff, per NFPA 90A. Include annual testing in O&M instructions.

Construct and seal ductwork per SMACNA and {{code.fecc}}; sealed against leakage with non-hardening, non-migrating sealant. Duct dimensions shown are inside-clear. Provide flexible connections at equipment.
Flexible duct: maximum length {{flexDuctMaxLen}}; centerline bend radius ≥ 1.5 diameters; sag ≤ ½" per foot; supported ≤ 5'-0" on-center without constriction.
Field-verify duct routing and coordinate with other services and ceiling elevations.
Provide a volume damper at every branch, outlet, and takeoff; mark open/closed positions with a durable locking quadrant.

Provide access panels for dampers, detectors, and serviceable devices concealed above non-accessible (gypsum/plaster) ceilings; fire-rating to match the penetrated assembly, minimum 1 hour. Identify each with a hanging marker.

Provide pressure-relief doors in supply and return ductwork for systems with VAV boxes, motorized dampers, or fire/smoke dampers, sized to protect the system during damper closure. Basis of design: Ruskin PRD-18 / NRD-18 or equal.

Outdoor-air intakes ≥ 10'-0" from any exhaust outlet or vent (unless the vent is ≥ 2' above the intake). Screen intakes and outlets (¼"–½" mesh). Provide motorized dampers on outdoor-air ducts, opening in occupied mode.
Exhaust and ventilation ducts: smooth-interior metal, terminating at an exterior cap or hood; provide back-draft dampers at fans. Dryer-exhaust ductwork: rigid metal, supported ≤ 4' on-center.
{{exhaustSpecial}}
clauses.note: Preselected from CMS exhaust data, editable. Type-specific clauses (grease, fume, toilet flex-metal exception) render here.

clauses.body: Provide diffusers, registers, and grilles coordinated with ceiling type and finish; verify locations against architectural reflected-ceiling plans and resolve conflicts before ordering. Seal register boxes airtight at the connection.
Keep openings covered after rough-in; do not operate the system before final startup.

Provide factory-assembled, factory-tested, UL-listed {{systemType}} equipment selected to meet: cooling ≥ {{loadcalc.coolingTons}} tons; heating ≥ {{loadcalc.heatingBtuh}} Btu/h by {{heatType}}; supply ≥ {{loadcalc.supplyCFM}} CFM; minimum efficiency {{seer2}} SEER2. Basis of design: {{manufacturer}} or equal.
Off-the-shelf listed equipment is factory-engineered. The Engineer's services cover system design and application of listed equipment, not the internal design of the listed equipment.
clauses.note: Jurisdiction-requested disclaimer, reworded. PRESCRIPTIVE/legal — keep verbatim.

clauses.body: Mount on {{acMounting}}. {{roofCurbSentence}}
clauses.note: roofCurbSentence auto-fills 'For roof-mounted equipment, set on a factory curb at the height required by [code.roofCurbTable].' only when acMounting = RTU/roof; blank otherwise.

clauses.body: Provide vibration isolation preventing objectionable transmission to structure or occupied space. {{dxProtectionSentence}}
clauses.note: dxProtectionSentence auto-fills 'Provide DX-equipment protection — anti-short-cycle, head- and pressure-controls — and electric-heat capacities per schedule.' UNLESS heatType = heat pump.

clauses.body: Filtration: serviceable section accepting standard filters; provide {{spareFilterSets}} spare set(s) at closeout.

Condensate: drain to an approved point, trapped and sized per {{code.fmc}}, insulated within conditioned space; corrosion-resistant, code-accepted material. Provide drain-pan safety cut-off; prove at startup.
Refrigerant piping: size and route per manufacturer requirements; weatherproof jackets where exposed; sleeve and protect where below-grade or through foundations per {{code.fpc}}.

Install per manufacturer instructions and the equipment listing; where these conflict with the Contract Documents, request clarification before proceeding. Coordinate routing and offsets to avoid conflicts with other trades.
At closeout, clean coils, pans, and drains; replace filters and belts; provide record documents and Owner training on operation and maintenance.
